claims.claim: Evidence from observational studies generally does not indicate a significant association between dietary cholesterol and cardiovascular disease risk.
Most meta-analyses of intervention studies associate cholesterol intakes exceeding current average levels with elevated total or low-density lipoprotein cholesterol concentrations.
Dietary guidance should focus on healthy dietary patterns (e.g., Mediterranean-style and DASH-style diets) that are relatively low in cholesterol.
Guidance focused on dietary patterns is more likely to improve diet quality and promote cardiovascular health than specific dietary cholesterol targets.
